title: OpenAI details Deployment Simulation for testing model behavior before launch
friendlyTitle: OpenAI is testing models with more realistic practice runs
summary: OpenAI described Deployment Simulation, a safety-evaluation method that replays real prior conversations in a privacy-preserving way and regenerates responses with a candidate model before release. The company says it helped estimate undesired behavior rates and surface issues in GPT-5-series deployments, including agentic settings.
friendlySummary: OpenAI shared a way to test new models by replaying real kinds of conversations before release. This helps spot problems before users run into them.
whyItMatters: As models become more agentic, pre-launch testing needs to resemble real usage instead of only synthetic red-team prompts. Better deployment forecasting could reduce everyday failures for users and give enterprises clearer risk signals before adopting new models.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Better testing can make AI tools safer and less surprising when they are used in normal work.
